//: Reviewed escapes for the test-quality gate (tools/check-test-quality.mjs): tests that are deliberately DISABLED (.skip/.todo/xit) or that deliberately carry no assertion. Keyed by '<file>::<test title>' — the TITLE, not a line number, so an entry survives the file being reformatted or the test moving. A reason is MANDATORY: a test that does not run is a hole in the net that every coverage number still counts as covered, so leaving one is a decision, not an oversight. There is NO escape for `.only` — a focused test silently disables the entire rest of the suite, and no reason makes that acceptable in a commit. This file is write-guard-protected and hashed by gate-integrity. A stale entry (its file is gone — e.g. an opt-in module this tier does not install) is ignored, not an error.

allow.reason: The RLS suite's documented self-skip, and it FAILS CLOSED where it matters. These four suites need a real Postgres; `node tests/rls/run-rls.mjs` fresh-applies the migrations and sets RLS_SUITE_READY=1, and in CI a skip is an ERROR (CI=true makes the runner fail closed). So the skipped branch exists only so that a plain `vitest run` on a laptop with no database stays green instead of erroring — it is the local-dev affordance, not a disabled test. The enforcing branch is the one CI runs.
Same RLS self-skip as cross-tenant-isolation: real-Postgres suite, green-without-a-database locally, fail-closed in CI via tests/rls/run-rls.mjs.

allow.test: tests/rls/hitl-transition-matrix.test.ts::HITL transition matrix — database enforcement (skipped: database not ready)
allow.reason: Same RLS self-skip as cross-tenant-isolation: real-Postgres suite, green-without-a-database locally, fail-closed in CI via tests/rls/run-rls.mjs.

allow.test: tests/rls/plan-regression.test.ts::RLS plan regression (skipped: database not ready)
allow.reason: Same RLS self-skip as cross-tenant-isolation: the DAL query-plan probe EXPLAINs against real Postgres at seed scale, so it cannot run without a database. Fail-closed in CI via tests/rls/run-rls.mjs.

allow.test: e2e/matrix.spec.ts::matrix route (skipped: not registered in this consumer)
allow.reason: A data-driven placeholder, and it has to exist. The matrix specs are gated on the app actually registering a 'matrix' ROUTES entry; a consumer who deleted the grid exemplar has nothing to assert about. The spec FILE must still declare at least one test, because tools/check-e2e.mjs asserts the spec set is neither vacuous nor truncated — so this is the honest way to say 'nothing to test here', rather than deleting the file and quietly shrinking the e2e surface.

allow.test: apps/server/src/observability/span-routes.test.ts::emits exactly one server span per request, named from this manifest (wire @opentelemetry/sdk-node + an InMemorySpanExporter, request each route, assert one span with the manifest name)
allow.reason: observability module: a deliberate, titled placeholder. Asserting real span emission needs @opentelemetry/sdk-node + an InMemorySpanExporter wired into the test host, which the module does not ship (it ships the manifest and the route-name contract). The title IS the specification for whoever wires the SDK. Tracked in docs/modules/observability/README.md.

allow.test: apps/server/src/observability/span-routes.test.ts::propagates pino log correlation (trace_id/span_id appear on request-scoped log lines via instrumentation-pino)
allow.reason: observability module: same placeholder class as above — proving trace/log correlation requires the OTel SDK and instrumentation-pino to be live in-process. Tracked in docs/modules/observability/README.md.
